post.description:
Note: Authorization in this context refers to role assignments, not to OAuth authorization.

 ActivateAuthorization activates an existing but inactive authorization.

 In case the authorization is already active, the request will return a successful response as
 the desired state is already achieved.
 You can check the change date in the response to verify if the authorization was activated by the request.

 Required permissions:
   - "user.grant.write"

Note: Authorization in this context refers to role assignments, not to OAuth authorization.

 CreateAuthorization creates a new authorization for a user in an owned or granted project.

 Required permissions:
   - "user.grant.write"

DeactivateAuthorization deactivates an existing and active authorization.

 Note: Authorization in this context refers to role assignments, not to OAuth authorization.

 In case the authorization is already inactive, the request will return a successful response as
 the desired state is already achieved.
 You can check the change date in the response to verify if the authorization was deactivated by the request.

 Required permissions:
   - "user.grant.write"

Note: Authorization in this context refers to role assignments, not to OAuth authorization.

 DeleteAuthorization deletes the authorization.

 In case the authorization is not found, the request will return a successful response as
 the desired state is already achieved.
 You can check the deletion date in the response to verify if the authorization was deleted by the request.

 Required permissions:
   - "user.grant.delete"

Note: Authorization in this context refers to role assignments, not to OAuth authorization.

 ListAuthorizations returns all authorizations matching the request and necessary permissions.

 Required permissions:
   - "user.grant.read"
   - no permissions required for listing own authorizations

Note: Authorization in this context refers to role assignments, not to OAuth authorization.

 UpdateAuthorization updates the authorization.

 Note that any role keys previously granted to the user and not present in the request will be revoked.

 Required permissions:
   - "user.grant.write"

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is no limit on the number of messages.

type.description: A URL that acts as a globally unique identifier for the type of the serialized message. For example: `type.googleapis.com/google.rpc.ErrorInfo`. This is used to determine the schema of the data in the `value` field and is the discriminator for the `debug` field.

value.description: The Protobuf message, serialized as bytes and base64-encoded. The specific message type is identified by the `type` field.

debug.title: Debug
debug.description: Deserialized error detail payload. The 'type' field indicates the schema. This field is for easier debugging and should not be relied upon for application logic.

connect.error_details.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message, with an additional debug field for ConnectRPC error details.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the authorization that should be activated.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the last timestamp when the authorization was changed / activated.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the authorization.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the authorization was created.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
ChangeDate is the timestamp when the authorization was last updated.
 In case the authorization was not updated, this field is equal to the creation date.

project.title: project
project.description: The project the user was granted the authorization for.

organization.title: organization
organization.description:
The organization the user was granted the authorization for.
 This does not have to correspond with the project or user's organization.
 But does represent the organization to which the authorization belongs and
 the user was given permissions for.

user.title: user
user.description: The user who was granted the authorization.

state.title: state
state.description: State is the current state of the authorization.

roles.title: roles
roles.description: Roles contains the roles the user was granted for the project.

authorizationIds.title: authorization_ids
authorizationIds.description: Search for authorizations by their IDs.

inUserIds.title: in_user_ids
inUserIds.description: Search for authorizations by the IDs of the users who were granted the authorizations.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: Search for authorizations by the ID of the organization it was granted for.

projectGrantId.title: project_grant_id
projectGrantId.description:
Search for authorizations by the ID of the project grant the user was granted the authorization for.
 This will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description:
Search for authorizations by the ID of the project the user was granted the authorization for.
 This will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

projectName.title: project_name
projectName.description:
Search for authorizations by the name of the project the user was granted the authorization for.
 This will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

roleKey.title: role_key
roleKey.description: Search for authorizations by the key of the role the user was granted.

state.title: state
state.description: Search for authorizations by their state.

userDisplayName.title: user_display_name
userDisplayName.description: Search for authorizations by the public display name of the granted user.

userOrganizationId.title: user_organization_id
userOrganizationId.description: Search for authorizations by the ID of the organisation the user is part of.

userPreferredLoginName.title: user_preferred_login_name
userPreferredLoginName.description: Search for authorizations by the preferred login name of the granted user.

userId.title: user_id
userId.description: UserID is the ID of the user who should be granted the authorization.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: Project ID is the ID of the project the user should be authorized for.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description:
OrganizationID is the ID of the organization on which the authorization should be created.
 The organization must either own the project or have a grant for the project.

roleKeys.title: role_keys
roleKeys.description: RoleKeys are the keys of the roles the user should be granted.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the newly created authorization.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the authorization was created.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the authorization that should be deactivated.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the last timestamp when the authorization was changed / deactivated.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the authorization that should be deleted.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp when the authorization was deleted.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Paginate through the results using a limit, offset and sorting.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
The field the result is sorted by. The default is the creation date.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Define the criteria to query for.

authorizations.title: authorizations
authorizations.description: Authorizations contains the list of authorizations matching the request.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the organization.

name.title: name
name.description: Name is the name of the organization.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the project.

name.title: name
name.description: Name is the name of the project.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description:
OrganizationID is the ID of the organization the project belongs to.
 This does not have to correspond with the authorizations organization.
 In case the authorization is given on a granted project, this is the organization
 that owns the project and granted it.

name.title: name
name.description:
Specify the name of the project the user was granted the authorization for to search for.
 Note that this will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the project name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations granted on a project with
 a name containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

key.title: key
key.description:
Key is the unique key of the role. It's the only relevant attribute for Zitadel and
 will be used for authorization checks and as claim in tokens and user info responses.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: Human readable name for the role, which might be displayed to users.

group.title: group
group.description: The group the role belongs to. This is used to group roles in the UI.

key.title: key
key.description: Specify the key of the role the user was granted to search for.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the role key. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations starting with a specific role key,
 use STARTS_WITH or STARTS_WITH_IGNORE_CASE.

state.title: state
state.description: Specify the state of the authorization to search for.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the authorization.

roleKeys.title: role_keys
roleKeys.description:
RoleKeys are the keys of the roles the user should be granted.
 Note that any role keys previously granted to the user and not present in the list will be revoked.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp when the authorization was last updated.

id.title: id
id.description: ID represents the ID of the user who was granted the authorization.

preferredLoginName.title: preferred_login_name
preferredLoginName.description: PreferredLoginName represents the preferred login name of the granted user.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: DisplayName represents the public display name of the granted user.

avatarUrl.title: avatar_url
avatarUrl.description: AvatarURL is the URL to the user's public avatar image.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description:
The organization the user belong to.
 This does not have to correspond with the authorizations organization.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: Specify the public display name of the granted user to search for.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the display name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations granted to a user with
 a display name containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

loginName.title: login_name
loginName.description: Specify the preferred login name of the granted user to search for.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the preferred login name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations granted to a user with
 a preferred login name containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

id.title: id
id.description: Only return resources that belong to this id.

ids.title: ids
ids.description: Defines the ids to query for.

offset.title: offset
offset.description: Starting point for retrieval, in combination of offset used to query a set list of objects.

limit.title: limit
limit.description:
limit is the maximum amount of objects returned. The default is set to 100
 with a maximum of 1000 in the runtime configuration.
 If the limit exceeds the maximum configured Zitadel will throw an error.
 If no limit is present the default is taken.

asc.title: asc
asc.description:
Asc is the sorting order. If true the list is sorted ascending, if false
 the list is sorted descending. The default is descending.

totalResult.title: total_result
totalResult.description: Absolute number of objects matching the query, regardless of applied limit.

appliedLimit.title: applied_limit
appliedLimit.description: Applied limit from query, defines maximum amount of objects per request, to compare if all objects are returned.

tags.name: zitadel.authorization.v2.AuthorizationService
tags.description:
Note: We have streamlined our naming conventions to improve clarity. Authorization in this context refers to role assignments, not to OAuth authorization.

 AuthorizationService provides methods to manage authorizations for users within your projects and applications.

 For managing permissions and roles for Zitadel internal resources, like organizations, projects,
 users, etc., please use the InternalPermissionService.
